questions.meaning: smiled radiantly and happily
questions.sample_sentence: I beamed with joy when Aparna ma'am declared that I had got the highest mark in maths in the second term in class 6.

questions.meaning: touching gently and lovingly
questions.sample_sentence: When I was caressing the dog Nandi, it quietly lay by my side.

questions.meaning: extremely ugly or unpleasant to look at
questions.sample_sentence: A car that had fallen into a caved-in road near Tidel Park looked hideous.

questions.meaning: elaborately decorated with intricate designs
questions.sample_sentence: Patri Satish Kumar's mridangams are ornate with special designs carved on them.

questions.meaning: the basic unit of British currency
questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Vishwak bought me a shirt that cost him 50 pounds.

questions.meaning: a person who sells goods from a market stall
questions.sample_sentence: The stallholder at Fruit Shop on Greams Road during the Sishya school Christmas carnival forgot to return the balance of ten rupees to me.

questions.meaning: very many; indefinitely numerous
questions.sample_sentence: The teacher sang the musical phrase umpteen times as the student was not able to understand it.

questions.meaning: old objects valued for their age, beauty, or rarity
questions.sample_sentence: During our family vacation, we visited a shop full of antiques including old bronze lamps and wooden carvings.

questions.meaning: looking closely or with difficulty
questions.sample_sentence: I was peering at the Swiggy app trying to find the best biryani restaurant near our house.

questions.meaning: negotiating the price of something
questions.sample_sentence: My father was bargaining with the stallholder at the Marina Beach market to get a better price for the cricket bat.

questions.meaning: a collection of valuable things
questions.sample_sentence: My collection of signed CSK merchandise is a real treasure that I keep safely in my cupboard.

questions.meaning: the act of finding something new or hidden
questions.sample_sentence: My discovery of a shortcut from Sishya school to the Greams Road bus stop saved me ten minutes every day.

questions.meaning: worth a lot of money or very useful
questions.sample_sentence: The tips my Dhrupad teacher gives me about breath control are extremely valuable for improving my singing.

questions.meaning: greatly surprised or astonished
questions.sample_sentence: Yohann was amazed when I told him that Sastha Catering Services had prepared idiappam and dosa for over five hundred people at the function.

questions.meaning: a large amount of money or wealth
questions.sample_sentence: My father joked that we spent a fortune ordering food from Swiggy during the summer holidays.

questions.meaning: truly what it is said to be; authentic
questions.sample_sentence: Vishwak helped me check whether the autographed CSK jersey I bought online was genuine or a fake.

questions.meaning: having strong feelings or enthusiasm
questions.sample_sentence: I am passionate about mridangam and hope to perform on stage with Patri Satish Kumar someday.

questions.meaning: graceful and stylish in appearance
questions.sample_sentence: The stage setup for the Dhrupad concert near Marina Beach looked elegant with flowers and traditional lamps.

questions.meaning: having sharp judgement; clever in practical matters
questions.sample_sentence: My friend Suvasana made a shrewd decision by trading her lunch snack for two dosas from the school canteen.

questions.meaning: the period after one stops working
questions.sample_sentence: My grandfather enjoyed his retirement by listening to Dhrupad music and taking long walks near Marina Beach every morning.

questions.meaning: quick and energetic
questions.sample_sentence: Yohann and I took a brisk walk around the school ground at Sishya before our cricket practice began.

questions.meaning: extremely beautiful or impressive
questions.sample_sentence: The mridangam concert at the Mylapore temple was truly magnificent, and even Patri Satish Kumar praised the young performer.

questions.meaning: a painting or photograph of a person
questions.sample_sentence: We have a large portrait of Shri Ramana Maharshi in our puja room that Appa brought from Tiruvannamalai.

questions.meaning: a public sale where items go to the highest bidder
questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Vishwak and I attended a charity auction at Sishya where old cricket bats were sold for large amounts.

questions.meaning: a strong cloth used for painting on
questions.sample_sentence: During art class at Sishya, we painted on a large canvas using watercolours to create a landscape of Marina Beach.

questions.meaning: waste material; things of no value
questions.sample_sentence: Amma scolded me for leaving rubbish on the dining table after eating biryani and watching the IPL match.

questions.meaning: large or generous (amount)
questions.sample_sentence: The stallholder at the Sishya Christmas carnival made a handsome profit by selling homemade snacks and filter coffee.
